member.name: T:Mafi.Unity.Audio.AudioChannel
member.summary: Represents IDs of groups in Unity's audio mixer.
member.remarks:
IDs must have default values since they are used as indices to arrays
If you are adding new channel here you need to create exposed variable via Unity. The name of the variable must exactly match "{ChannelName}Volume" (right click on volume of the channel in mixer, expose, then rename). See https://docs.unity3d.com/Manual/AudioMixerInspectors.html

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.Audio.EntitySoundMb.buildLogRolloffCurve(System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Builds a custom rolloff curve: volume halves at each distance doubling (log-like), with the last segment fading to zero at maxDistance instead of hard-cutting. Tangents are derived from vol(x) = m/x, so vol'(x) = -m/x².

member.name: T:Mafi.Unity.Camera.CubicBezierCurve3Float
member.summary: Float-based cubic Bezier curve with arc-length parameterized sampling. Unlike which uses Fix32/Fix64 for determinism, this class uses plain floats since it's only used for UI/camera work.

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.Camera.CubicBezierCurve3Float.Build(UnityEngine.Vector3[],System.Int32)
member.summary: Builds the curve from a list of control points. Control points: [start, cp1, cp2, end, cp1, cp2, end, ...] = 1 + 3*segments.

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.Camera.CubicBezierCurve3Float.SampleAtArcLength(System.Single)
member.summary: Samples the curve position at a given arc-length.

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.Camera.CubicBezierCurve3Float.SampleTangentAtArcLength(System.Single)
member.summary: Samples the curve derivative (tangent) at a given arc-length.

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.Camera.CubicBezierCurve3Float.ComputeSegmentLength(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Computes arc-length of a single segment by polyline approximation.

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.Camera.CubicBezierCurve3Float.getCurveLocation(System.Single,System.Int32@,System.Single@)
member.summary: Converts arc-length to (segmentIndex, localT) using binary search on the arc-length table.

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.Camera.CubicBezierCurve3Float.sampleSegment(System.Int32,System.Single)
member.summary: Evaluates cubic Bezier at parameter t in [0,1] for a segment starting at control point basePtIndex. Standard formula: B(t) = (1-t)^3*P0 + 3t(1-t)^2*P1 + 3t^2(1-t)*P2 + t^3*P3

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.Camera.CubicBezierCurve3Float.sampleSegmentDerivative(System.Int32,System.Single)
member.summary: Evaluates the derivative of cubic Bezier at parameter t. B'(t) = 3(1-t)^2*(P1-P0) + 6t(1-t)*(P2-P1) + 3t^2*(P3-P2)

member.name: T:Mafi.Unity.Camera.DollyCameraModel
member.summary: Camera model that plays back dolly tracks. Driven by arc-length along a with per-knot interpolation of yaw, pitch, FOV, and speed. During playback, children of a configurable parent GameObject are shown/hidden based on per-knot visibility sets (AND of adjacent knots, updated on segment change).

member.name: E:Mafi.Unity.Camera.DollyCameraModel.PlaybackStopped
member.summary: Fired when playback finishes or is cancelled. Bool indicates whether it was cancelled.

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.Camera.DollyCameraModel.StartPlayback(Mafi.Core.Camera.DollyTrackData,System.Int32)
member.summary: Rebuilds the curve, resolves the visibility parent, and begins advancing along the track.

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.Camera.DollyCameraModel.RestoreObjectVisibility
member.summary: Restores all children under the visibility parent to active. Call this when the editor UI is shown again after playback, not immediately when playback stops.

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.Camera.DollyCameraModel.updateObjectVisibility
member.summary: Shows/hides children of the visibility parent based on the two knots of the current segment. Only re-evaluates when the segment index changes. A child is visible only if named in both knots' VisibleObjects (AND logic); an empty set means all hidden.

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.Camera.DollyCameraModel.ParseVisibleNamesInto(System.String,System.Collections.Generic.HashSet{System.String})
member.summary: Parses a comma/semicolon/newline-separated list of visible object names into . Clears the set first. Empty or null input results in an empty set (all hidden).

member.name: T:Mafi.Unity.Camera.DollyTrackCurve
member.summary: Wraps with Catmull-Rom-to-Bezier conversion and arc-length parameterized sampling for dolly camera tracks. Position is always C1 (Catmull-Rom to Bezier). Per-knot scalar properties (yaw, pitch, speed, FOV) can use either Catmull-Rom (C1) or natural cubic splines (C2) depending on .

member.name: F:Mafi.Unity.Camera.DollyTrackCurve.m_knotArcLengths
member.summary: Arc-length at each knot boundary (index 0 = 0, index i = arc-length up to knot i).

member.name: F:Mafi.Unity.Camera.DollyTrackCurve.m_unwrappedYaw
member.summary: Unwrapped yaw and pitch values: consecutive knots always take the shortest angular path. This avoids the C1 discontinuity that per-segment DeltaAngle normalization causes when the "shortest path" wrapping chooses different directions in adjacent segments.

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.Camera.DollyTrackCurve.Rebuild(Mafi.Core.Camera.DollyTrackData)
member.summary: Rebuilds the curve from knots using Catmull-Rom tangent estimation converted to cubic Bezier. Requires at least 2 knots.

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.Camera.DollyTrackCurve.GetArcLengthAtKnot(System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the arc-length at the start of the given knot index.

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.Camera.DollyTrackCurve.GetSegmentAtArcLength(System.Single)
member.summary: Returns the segment index for a given arc-length. The camera is between knot and knot segIndex + 1. Requires at least 2 knots and a valid curve.

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.Camera.DollyTrackCurve.InterpolateKnotProperties(Mafi.Core.Camera.DollyTrackData,System.Single,System.Single@,System.Single@,System.Single@,System.Single@)
member.summary: Interpolates per-knot properties (yaw, pitch, speed multiplier, FOV) at a given arc-length.

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.Camera.DollyTrackCurve.EstimateTravelTime(Mafi.Core.Camera.DollyTrackData)
member.summary: Estimates total travel time by simulating playback forward in small time steps, including ease-in/out (smootherstep) and per-knot speed multipliers.

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.Camera.DollyTrackCurve.SamplePolyline(System.Int32)
member.summary: Samples the curve at regular intervals for visualization.

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.Camera.DollyTrackCurve.catmullRom(System.Single,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Non-uniform Catmull-Rom (cubic Hermite) interpolation for scalar values. Tangents are scaled by the ratio of current segment arc-length to the arc-length span between outer knots, ensuring C1 continuity in arc-length space even when segments have different lengths.

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.Camera.DollyTrackCurve.besselStartTangent(UnityEngine.Vector3[],System.Int32)
member.summary: Bessel tangent at the first endpoint: derivative of the quadratic through the first 3 points.

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.Camera.DollyTrackCurve.besselEndTangent(UnityEngine.Vector3[],System.Int32)
member.summary: Bessel tangent at the last endpoint: derivative of the quadratic through the last 3 points.

member.name: T:Mafi.Unity.Camera.DollyTrackCurve.NaturalCubicSpline
member.summary: Natural cubic spline for scalar interpolation with C2 continuity. Natural boundary conditions (second derivative = 0 at endpoints). Coefficients are in the form: S(x) = a + b*(x-xi) + c*(x-xi)^2 + d*(x-xi)^3

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.Camera.DollyTrackCurve.NaturalCubicSpline.Build(Mafi.Collections.Lyst{System.Single},Mafi.Collections.Lyst{System.Single},System.Int32)
member.summary: Builds the spline from knot x-positions and values. Solves the tridiagonal system for natural boundary conditions in O(n) time.

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.Camera.DollyTrackCurve.NaturalCubicSpline.Build(Mafi.Collections.Lyst{System.Single},System.Single[],System.Int32)
member.summary: Builds the spline from knot x-positions and values array.

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.Camera.DollyTrackCurve.NaturalCubicSpline.Evaluate(Mafi.Collections.Lyst{System.Single},System.Int32,System.Single)
member.summary: Evaluates the spline at the given x using the precomputed segment index.

member.name: T:Mafi.Unity.Camera.DollyTrackEditor
member.summary: Editor UI and input handling for dolly camera tracks. Manages track/knot CRUD, 3D visualization (curve polyline, knot handles, direction lines), RTG transform gizmos, and clipboard import/export. Activated from photo mode; drives for playback preview.

member.name: F:Mafi.Unity.Camera.DollyTrackEditor.EDITOR_UI_LAYER
member.summary: Custom UI layer for the editor panel: below FLOATERS (5000) so dropdown/confirmation popups render on top, but canBeHidden=false so it stays visible in photo mode.

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.Camera.DollyTrackEditor.ProcessInput(System.Boolean@)
member.summary: Called each frame from PhotoModeController. Returns true if input was consumed.

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.Camera.DollyTrackEditor.applyWeatherOverride
member.summary: Applies the active track's visual weather override to the . Clears the override when not in edit mode, when no active track, or when the track's override string is empty or does not match any registered (e.g. mod removed - stored ID is preserved so it can be restored if the mod returns). The controller debounces identical targets in its next sync tick, so repeat calls are cheap.

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.Camera.DollyTrackEditor.refreshEditorUi
member.summary: Rebuilds the ObjEditor UI from the view model. ObjEditor.UpdateValues() only works when RenderUpdate is called by a dock/tab system, but we're standalone, so call SetObjectToEdit directly.

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.Camera.DollyTrackEditor.isTooCloseToNeighbors(UnityEngine.Vector3,System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns true if the given position is too close to any neighboring knot at the given index. Checks the knot before and after the insertion point.

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.Camera.DollyTrackEditor.computeInheritedVisibility(Mafi.Core.Camera.DollyTrackData,System.Int32)
member.summary: Computes the VisibleObjects value for a knot being inserted at the given index. Returns the intersection of the two neighbors' visible sets (non-disruptive insert). Returns empty string when appending or when both neighbors have no visibility set.

member.summary: Returns if it matches one of the dropdown options, else null. Ensures that what the UI shows ("No change" for unknown values) matches what applies.

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.Camera.OrbitalCameraModel.applyCursorCenteredZoomShift(Mafi.RelTile1f)
member.summary: Shifts the camera pivot so that the world point under the cursor stays visually fixed after a zoom change. Works by computing where the cursor ray hits a ground plane before and after the orbit radius change, then offsetting the pivot by the difference.

member.name: T:Mafi.Unity.Factory.Transports.DestroyMeshOnDestroy
member.summary: Transports use custom meshes. Each is attached to its mesh filter. Upon destroying the transport, the mesh stays around. This script gets attached to the transport GameObject to kill the mesh when the transport is killed. Without this Unity keeps those meshes around forever! NOTE: This does mean we can never share meshes between transports.

member.name: T:Mafi.Unity.Factory.Transports.TransportModelFactory
member.summary: Procedural generation of transport models.
member.remarks: This code is used for both the instanced and non-instanced paths. They diverge after cross sections are created. Transport models are generated on the fly in both instanced and non-instanced, with the hope that this will help future-proof against trajectories we may not support at the time of writing.

member.summary: Creates transport model with given pivots and start/end directions.

member.remarks: Dealing with "twist" of up vector on vertical pipes is a pain. If we wanted to simplify this code we could have an explicit graphical discontinuity on these sections which could then be used to hide anything but "normal" twist.

member.name: T:Mafi.Unity.InputControl.GameMenu.LoadSave.DlcInclusionWindow
member.summary: Small dialog shown when a player is about to load a save that does not include all DLCs they own, giving them the option to include them or skip.

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.InputControl.GameMenu.LoadSave.LoadWindow.onLoadClick
member.summary:
Load button click handler. Two distinct flows:
EnableUnloadingOfAnyMod flow (recovery saves): If DLCs or third-party mods are available, force-opens the mod selection screen so the player can freely add/remove any mod or DLC before loading. If none exist, loads directly. The DLC inclusion nag dialog is skipped because the mod picker already gives full control.
Normal flow: Shows a DLC inclusion prompt if any installed DLCs are unselected. Then proceeds through proceedWithLoad which checks for failed/new third-party mods and opens the mod edit window if needed, or loads directly.

member.name: F:Mafi.Unity.InstancedRendering.InstancedMeshesRenderer`1.LodPxPerMeter
member.summary: LOD-per-meter for each LOD level (values must be descending). The lower the number, the later the lod be shown. Value 0 means LOD will be shown at any distance. member.name: T:Mafi.Unity.InstancedRendering.ProductsRenderer
member.summary: Handles rendering for transported products.
member.remarks:
Notes from transport optimization (Change-Id: I9988f39e22133491718cbe14b8135a69ac022232)
Transported products are now rendered using efficient GPU-instancing that renders all products of one product type in one draw-call. - Preparation of data for the rendering is done on sim thread, but only when needed. If transport is stationary, update is skipped. - Transported product data for GPU shader are packed to 4 x 4 bytes struct (per product) to optimize memory access and have all 4B floats properly aligned. - Transported products shader has two variants: static and dynamic. Static renders products without movement and needs half of the data compared to dynamic variant, and avoids any position/rotation interpolation computation. Dynamic variant performs smooth interpolation between two sim steps for frame-rate independent smooth movement with no CPU interventions.
TODO: It would be possible to further optimize products renderer to upload ProductInstanceData[] on GPU and each product would just have an index to that array. This would reduce the data transfer between CPU and GPU 4x and lots of CPU time will be also saved by collecting 4x less data when positions change. Stacking would likely require to have 3 elements per each product (or we can have 3 positions and just one rotation to aid stacking).
Even further, each chunk/renderer could keep a separate buffer and if there are less than 65k rendered products, only ushort would be needed, further reducing the data amount by 2x.

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.InstancedRendering.ProductsRenderer.SwapLateDataAndUpload
member.summary: Swaps quaternion prepared data into draw buffers and uploads all pending GPU data. Called by at the end of RenderUpdateAfterSync, after all entity updaters have run. This ordering is required because late writers (e.g. truck flatbed attachments) compute product positions in RenderUpdateAfterSync using GPU-computed vehicle poses that are only available after sync.

member.name: F:Mafi.Unity.Terrain.Designation.TerrainDesignationsRenderer.TerrainDesignationsChunk.TerrainDesignationInstanceData.HeightAtCenter
member.summary: Height at

member.name: F:Mafi.Unity.Terrain.Designation.TerrainDesignationsRenderer.TerrainDesignationsChunk.TerrainDesignationInstanceData.PackedData
member.summary: Packed data. Includes warnings per edge. 1st bit is -Y edge. 2nd bit is +X edge. 3rd bit is +Y edge. 4th bit is -Y edge. 5th bit marks us as forestry. 6th bit is "self-compute colour", which also means "is mixed mining/dumping". 7th bit is "ready to be fulfilled". 8th to 32nd bits (inclusive) are a map of non-fulfilled tiles.

member.summary: Depth at the four corners: origin, +X, +XY, +Y.

member.summary: Color and depth at the center in the alpha channel.

member.name: T:Mafi.Unity.Terrain.TerrainRenderer
member.summary: Handles chunked and LODed rendering of: terrain, terrain details, tile surfaces, and ocean. This class handles all these at the same time to make them efficient since each uses similar infra for chunking, syncing, and rendering.

member.name: P:Mafi.Unity.Terrain.TerrainRenderer.UpdateDelayPerLod
member.summary: Terrain update frequency based on LOD, lat value is update frequency of chunks that are not visible. We still update chunks that are not visible to avoid huge visual jumps when camera jumps to a new position. The numbers are primes to help avoid updating too many chunks in the same frame when camera is stationary. IMPORTANT: It is critical that all values are greater than 2 to avoid race conflicts during update.

member.name: T:Mafi.Unity.Terrain.WaterRendererFft
member.summary: Renders Ocean using sum of large amounts of since waves. To make this efficient, inverse FFT and compute shaders are used. To avoid tiling, two cascades with different scales are simulated and then merged in shader.
member.remarks: Inspired by https://github.com/gasgiant/FFT-Ocean (MIT-licensed)

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.Terrain.WaterRendererFft.flushPendingChunkUpdates
member.summary: Drains every chunk that has pending changes in one pass - bypasses the normal per-frame throttle (MAX_CHANGED_TILES_PER_SIM / one-chunk-per-renderUpdate) so the caller can be sure the ocean is fully in sync with the current terrain state before returning. Intended to be called from the recovery save import path under the loading screen; otherwise the throttled async path is preferred.

member.summary: Returns true if this sound should be created. Returns false when the previous car in the train already plays the same sound, to reduce audio source count on long trains. By checking whether the previous car actually has the sound active (not just the prototype), we get an alternating pattern: if the previous car skipped a sound, this car will create it, and vice versa.

member.name: T:Mafi.Unity.UiToolkit.Component.CacheInComponent
member.summary:
Cache used inside components like Row or Column.
This class is capable of caching multiple types of views and does not need to be strongly typed which makes its usage very streamlined.
This cache is tied to an individual container (passed via ctor) and it uses children of the container to find used items and reuse them on clear. Since this cache does not track used items it is more lightweight and also does not risk memory leaks.

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.UiToolkit.Component.CacheInComponent.ReturnAll
member.summary: Adds all the views from the container that are compatible with this cache back to the cache. But due to performance reasons the views are not removed individually. The calling container has to clear its children to finalize the return. Another reason this method can't call clear on the container is because the container overrides clear and is already calling this method so that would cause infinite recursion.

member.name: T:Mafi.Unity.UiToolkit.Component.SimpleGapHandler
member.summary: Does gap properly but fails for components that wrap content.

member.name: T:Mafi.Unity.UiToolkit.Component.GridGapHandler
member.summary: Handles gap for containers that wrap and use gap in both axes. But as a side effect, it also introduces padding of 1/2 gap inside the container.

member.name: T:Mafi.Unity.UiToolkit.Library.FloatingPanel.FloatingColumn
member.summary:
Implements a floating panel that can float over the rest of the UI while attached to a target. It properly handles screen edges and is also able to follow a target that is moving.
Make sure to add this panel to a layer that is above the rest of the UI. The parent layer should occupy all the area that is available for the window to use. In case of tooltips, such parent would span the entire screen.
You can define its placement policy by passing .
Note: This is more of a low level component. For simple one-time adhoc floating containers look into .

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.UiToolkit.Library.FloatingPanel.FloatingColumn.setGeometryCallback(System.Boolean)
member.summary:
Note keeping the callback on, all the time might make us react to our changes causing recursion in Unity. Happens only when scrolling near the edge where tooltip has to flip over. It is not clear why as we are not changing our size, just choosing a position.
However, having callback for the first layout is important as it might not happen on the next frame.

member.name: T:Mafi.Unity.UiToolkit.Library.FloatingPanel.FloatingColumnPromiseBase`1
member.summary: Floating panels such as tooltips typically have very small number or real instances. So having a UI for each use would unnecessarily increase size of the DOM. For that reason we just cache the instances via UiBuilder and reuse them. This saves thousands of unnecessary elements.

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.UiToolkit.Library.FloatingPanel.FloatingPanelExtensions.Floater``1(``0,System.Func{Mafi.Option{Mafi.Unity.UiToolkit.Component.UiComponent}},Mafi.Unity.UiToolkit.Library.FloatingPanel.IFloatingPositionPolicy,System.Boolean)
member.summary:
Provides content placed into a floating container styled like a panel.
Implement the function to provide content for the panel. The function is called each time the panel is about to be displayed. The panel is cached but the content is not; it is caller's responsibility to cache it, if needed. You can listen to OnDetach on the content's UiComponent to know that the container got hidden and can be recycled.
Position may be specified by FloaterPositionPolicy.BELOW, FloaterPositionPolicy.ABOVE (default)

member.name: T:Mafi.Unity.UiToolkit.Library.Window
member.summary: How to size your window: - Always set the size using WindowSize() or Fullscreen() APIs (do not use Size()). - Do not rely on default behavior, always set size params that make sense for your use-case - If you set Px size into WindowSize it will set that size as the Max size. So increase the size until you the window no longer benefits from getting more space. Don't worry about the min-size, windows can always shrink. - Never try to set a min size, some players run very small resolutions (e.g. 1280x800 plus scale). We prefer squished content over partially visible windows. That's why WindowSize treats Px as max size. Other tips for windows size: - You can use Px.Auto for window size. The window will expand based on its content but will still be able to shrink on small screen. But avoid auto size, if you have: - Dynamic content that can cause the window to jump back and forth - Fixed size containers that are not in a scroller -> these can make the window too big on small screens - For multi-pane windows, use percentages for pane's sizes (not fixed pixels) to allow the window to shrink

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.UiToolkit.Library.Window.WindowSize(Mafi.Px,Mafi.Px)

More details on window sizing are in the Window class documentation.

member.name: T:Mafi.Unity.Ui.Controllers.LayoutEntityPlacing.StaticEntityMassPlacer.DragPlacementHelper
member.summary:
Handles click+drag multi-placement of layout entities and transports along a line or grid. Supports both single entities and multi-entity blueprints including transports. Layout previews are repositioned via SetTransform; transport previews are destroyed and recreated with offset trajectories when positions change.
Cancellation: right-click during drag cancels the drag and keeps the tool open (suppressing both the secondary-up and the subsequent primary-up from the held left button). Short clicks (under ) fall through to normal single placement.

member.name: F:Mafi.Unity.Ui.Controllers.LayoutEntityPlacing.StaticEntityMassPlacer.DragPlacementHelper.m_groupSize
member.summary: Number of entity previews per stamp (1 for single entity, N for blueprints).

member.name: F:Mafi.Unity.Ui.Controllers.LayoutEntityPlacing.StaticEntityMassPlacer.DragPlacementHelper.m_basePreviewCount
member.summary: Count of original (non-drag) previews in m_entityPreviews at drag start. Drag previews occupy indices [m_basePreviewCount .. m_entityPreviews.Count).

member.name: F:Mafi.Unity.Ui.Controllers.LayoutEntityPlacing.StaticEntityMassPlacer.DragPlacementHelper.m_entityTransforms
member.summary: Original transforms of layout entities, captured at drag start. For transports, this stores a default transform (position only, from config or pivot).

member.name: F:Mafi.Unity.Ui.Controllers.LayoutEntityPlacing.StaticEntityMassPlacer.DragPlacementHelper.m_entityTrajectories
member.summary: Original trajectories for transport entities. None for layout entities.

member.name: F:Mafi.Unity.Ui.Controllers.LayoutEntityPlacing.StaticEntityMassPlacer.DragPlacementHelper.m_entityPillarHints
member.summary: Original pillar hints for transport entities. Empty for layout entities.

member.name: F:Mafi.Unity.Ui.Controllers.LayoutEntityPlacing.StaticEntityMassPlacer.DragPlacementHelper.m_selectionSize
member.summary: Bounding box of the selection, used for spacing computation.

member.name: F:Mafi.Unity.Ui.Controllers.LayoutEntityPlacing.StaticEntityMassPlacer.DragPlacementHelper.m_overlapDeltaX
member.summary: How many tiles the bounding box step is reduced by along each axis. Clamped to selection size.

member.name: F:Mafi.Unity.Ui.Controllers.LayoutEntityPlacing.StaticEntityMassPlacer.DragPlacementHelper.m_hasTransports
member.summary: Whether the selection contains any transport entities.

member.name: F:Mafi.Unity.Ui.Controllers.LayoutEntityPlacing.StaticEntityMassPlacer.DragPlacementHelper.m_lastStampPositions
member.summary: Last stamp positions used, for detecting when transport previews need recreation.

member.name: F:Mafi.Unity.Ui.Controllers.LayoutEntityPlacing.StaticEntityMassPlacer.DragPlacementHelper.m_dragPreviews
member.summary: Extra previews created during drag, organized as groups of m_groupSize.

member.name: P:Mafi.Unity.Ui.Controllers.LayoutEntityPlacing.StaticEntityMassPlacer.DragPlacementHelper.ShouldSuppressMouseUp
member.summary:
True after a drag was cancelled by right-click, until both mouse buttons have been released for a full frame. Suppresses both the secondary-up (tool close) and the primary-up (placement) that follow because the user was holding left mouse during the drag.
Scenario: user holds left mouse to drag, right-clicks to cancel, then releases left. Without suppression, the left-up would trigger unwanted placement at the cancelled position. The flag persists through the left-up frame via delayed clearing in , ensuring both up-events are suppressed regardless of whether they arrive on the same frame or different frames.

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.Ui.Controllers.LayoutEntityPlacing.StaticEntityMassPlacer.DragPlacementHelper.UpdateCancellationState
member.summary:
Clears the cancellation flag once both mouse buttons have been released for a full frame. Call once per frame, before any checks.
Uses a one-frame delay: when both buttons are up, we mark for clearing next frame rather than clearing immediately. This ensures the flag survives the frame where the last button is released (which has an up-event that still needs suppression). Covers both sequential release (right up, then left up) and simultaneous release.

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.Ui.Controllers.LayoutEntityPlacing.StaticEntityMassPlacer.DragPlacementHelper.computeAxisAlignedPositions(Mafi.Tile3i,Mafi.Tile3i,Mafi.Collections.Lyst{Mafi.Tile3i})
member.summary: Axis-aligned placement: snaps to the dominant axis (90-degree snapping) and spaces entities by the selection size along that axis.

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.Ui.Controllers.LayoutEntityPlacing.StaticEntityMassPlacer.DragPlacementHelper.computeFreeAnglePositions(Mafi.Tile3i,Mafi.Tile3i,Mafi.Collections.Lyst{Mafi.Tile3i})
member.summary: Free-angle placement: steps exactly by entity size on the constraining axis and linearly interpolates the other axis. Guarantees tight packing (no gaps) and no overlap.

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.Ui.Controllers.LayoutEntityPlacing.StaticEntityMassPlacer.DragPlacementHelper.computeGridPositions(Mafi.Tile3i,Mafi.Tile3i,Mafi.Collections.Lyst{Mafi.Tile3i})
member.summary: Grid placement: fills a rectangular grid with entities.

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.Ui.Controllers.LayoutEntityPlacing.StaticEntityMassPlacer.DragPlacementHelper.syncExtraPreviews(Mafi.Collections.Lyst{Mafi.Tile3i})
member.summary: Syncs extra stamp previews to match the given positions. For transport entities, previews are created at the correct offset position (they can't be repositioned). When positions change for existing stamps with transports, those previews are destroyed and recreated.

member.name: T:Mafi.Unity.UnityTag
member.summary: Helper enum of all common Unity tags. Use to test these tags.
member.remarks:
Since Unity does not support multi-tags, we workaround it with tags concatenated to string and define all needed combinations. Note that new tags and new used combinations has to be added in unity editor.
Tags are intentionally short since we test them with and testing usually happens multiple times per frame.

member.name: F:Mafi.Unity.UnityTag.NoHi

member.name: F:Mafi.Unity.UnityTag.NoOnlyHi
member.summary: No Highlight: Game object marked with this tag will not be highlighted. This does not prevent setting materials (e.g. making it blue until built).

member.name: F:Mafi.Unity.UnityTag.PiPa

member.name: F:Mafi.Unity.UnityTag.Persistent

member.name: T:Mafi.Unity.Utils.ActivatorState
member.summary: Encapsulates an activation state - active/inactive, that can be changed by multiple different sources. This class counts number of activations to allow overlapping calls. The activation itself is done through created using .

member.name: T:Mafi.Unity.Utils.AnimationSyncer
member.summary: Synchronizes animation with sim state. This uses speed-based synchronization that smoothly changes animation speed to match the sim state instead of jumping the animation every sync that introduces jitter. Another benefit is that calling every sync causes performance overhead.
member.remarks:
When using this syncer make sure that the first after already has non-zero targetNormTime.
If sim state counts animValue from 0 to n-1 (animation of length animLength = n), the correct formula for targetNormTime passed to is targetNormTime = (float)(animValue + 1) / targetNormTime.
This syncer still assumes that the animation speed in Unity is the same as speed in game sim. If the speed is too different, this class reverts to jumping to avoid crazy fast or crazy slow animations in case of "jumps" in animations state.

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.Utils.AnimationSyncer.Sync(System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Call every sync.

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.Utils.AnimationSyncer.PlayNew(System.Int32,System.Single)
member.summary: Starts new animation. Must be called BEFORE . This assumes that the animation was started at the beginning of the last sim update is already playing for one tick at this point.

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.Utils.AnimationSyncer.Stop
member.summary: Stops the animation. Similarly as this assumes that the animation was stopped at the start of last sim update (last sync was updated to a value near 1.0).

member.name: T:Mafi.Unity.Utils.CombinedActivator
member.summary: Combines multiple activators into one which affects all at once.

member.name: T:Mafi.Unity.Utils.CoroutineHelper
member.summary: Allows invoking coroutines from outside of MonoBehaviors.

member.name: T:Mafi.Unity.Utils.DelayedItemsProcessing`1
member.summary: Accepts additions and removals on the sim thread and properly synchronizes these events to be propagated to the render thread. The order of events is preserved.

member.name: T:Mafi.Unity.Utils.IActivator
member.summary: General interface for an activator of UI components (mainly for visualizations or UI tools). There may be several classes that want to activate/deactivate (show/hide) certain UI components, each of them gets one IActivator and activates the component through the activator. The activator allows for greater flexibility and error checks than simple Activate/Deactivate methods.

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.Utils.IActivator.Deactivate
member.summary: Deactivates the target component, logs an error, if the target component was not active.

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.Utils.IActivator.DeactivateIfActive
member.summary: Same as , but does not log errors.

member.name: T:Mafi.Unity.Utils.OptionalParticlesWrapper
member.summary: Convenient wrapper that hides the fact that particles might be missing. Definitely better than crashing the game or break rendering.

member.name: T:Mafi.Unity.Utils.ReloadAfterAssetUpdateManager
member.summary: Workaround for a Unity issue where material looses buffer after asset update. https://forum.unity.com/threads/shader-stops-rendering-when-assets-are-updated.1240933/

member.name: T:Mafi.Unity.Utils.ReversibleAnimationSyncer
member.summary:
Similar to for for reversible animations.
Note that speed mult parameter is used to reverse animations. It must be defined as a parameter on the AC and be set as speed multiplier on the animation clip. Since parameters are global for the entire AC, you will need to define more parameters if there are more reversible clips in the animator.
member.remarks: For no good reason, Unity does not support setting negative, but allows setting a custom animator parameter that is speed multiplier to negative number. This is the only way to achieve backwards playback.

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.Utils.ReversibleAnimationSyncer.Sync(System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Call every sync.

member.summary: Starts new animation. Must be called BEFORE . This assumes that the animation was started at the beginning of the last sim update is already playing for one tick at this point.

member.name: M:Mafi.Unity.Utils.ReversibleAnimationSyncer.Stop
member.summary: Stops the animation. Similarly as this assumes that the animation was stopped at the start of last sim update (last sync was updated to a value near 1.0).
Schedules a screenshot capture. The actual capture is done during render-update-end phase so that all instanced geometry is properly scheduled for rendering.
member.remarks: If file path is provided, screenshot will be saved in a separate thread to not block execution. If encodedDataCallback is provided instead, it is called synchronously in the render-update-end phase. Path and data callback should not be set at the same time.

member.name: P:Mafi.Unity.MeshBuilder.Instance
member.summary: Returns global clean instance for mesh construction. It is responsibility of the caller to clear the builder when he is doe with it! This instance should not be cached or stored and should be used to produce mesh within small scope. Returned builder is unique per thread. For more complicated mesh building please create your own instance.
member.remarks: Sharing one instance of a builder prevents re-allocation of all the buffers and reduces memory footprint compared to privately owned builders.

member.name: T:RTG.GizmoHandleCanHoverHandler
member.summary: Delegate which is used to handle CanHover events fired from the GizmoHandle class.
member.param: The handle id.
The gizmo which owns the handle.
Contains useful information about the handle hover state.
All handlers must answer with either yes or no to tell the handle if it can be hovered.

member.name: T:RTG.GizmoHandle
member.summary: Gizmo handles represent the core components that are used to define a gizmo. A handle is essentially a shape, or collection of shapes, both 2D and/or 3D, which can be tapped and interacted with by the user. A gizmo is, at its core, nothing else but a collection of handles.

member.name: F:RTG.GizmoHandle.CanHover
member.summary: Event fired from 'GetHoverData' to allow subscribers to decide if a handle is allowed to be hovered,
member.param: The handle id.
The gizmo which owns the handle.
Contains useful information about the handle hover state.
All handlers must answer with either yes or no to tell the handle if it can be hovered.

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoHandle.Id
member.summary: Returns the handle's id. The id is unique at gizmo scope, but handles that belong to different gizmos might have the same id.

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoHandle.Gizmo
member.summary: Returns the gizmo which owns the handle. Only one gizmo can own a handle and this gizmo must be specified at handle creation time as a constructor parameter. Thus, the owner gizmo can not be changed after the handle was created.

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoHandle.DragSession
member.summary: The drag session which is associated with the handle. When the user taps one of the gizmo handles, the gizmo will check if the handle has a drag session associated with it and if it does, it will attempt to start the session.

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoHandle.GenericHoverPriority
member.summary: Returns the handle's generic hover priority. The generic hover priority is used as a sorting criteria by the hover update algorithm to sort between a mixture of 2D and 3D handles that are hovered by the cursor.

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoHandle.HoverPriority2D
member.summary: Returns the handle's 2D hover priority. The 2D hover priority is used as a sorting criteria by the hover update algorithm to sort between 2D handles that are hovered by the cursor.

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoHandle.HoverPriority3D
member.summary: Returns the handle's 3D hover priority. The 2D hover priority is used as a sorting criteria by the hover update algorithm to sort between 3D handles that are hovered by the cursor.

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoHandle.Num3DShapes
member.summary: Returns the number of 3D shapes which are associated with the handle.

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoHandle.Num2DShapes
member.summary: Returns the number of 2D shapes which are associated with the handle.

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoHandle.Has3DShapes
member.summary: Can be used to check if the handle has any 3D shapes associated with it. Shorthand for checking if the number of 3D shapes is different than 0.

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoHandle.Has2DShapes
member.summary: Can be used to check if the handle has any 2D shapes associated with it. Shorthand for checking if the number of 2D shapes is different than 0.

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoHandle.Is2DHoverable
member.summary: This property can be used to tell the handle if its 2D shapes are hoverable. For example, if a handle contains both 3D and 2D shapes, setting this to false will cause the hover algorithm to ignore 2D shapes and consider only the 3D ones.

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoHandle.Is3DHoverable
member.summary: This property can be used to tell the handle if its 3D shapes are hoverable. For example, if a handle contains both 3D and 2D shapes, setting this to false will cause the hover algorithm to ignore 3D shapes and consider only the 2D ones.

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoHandle.Is2DVisible
member.summary: This property can be used to tell the handle that it should not render any of its 2D shapes. Note: When this property is set to false, any 2D shapes associated with the handle will no longer be hoverable even if 'Is2DHoverable' returns true.

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoHandle.Is3DVisible
member.summary: This property can be used to tell the handle that it should not render any of its 3D shapes. Note: When this property is set to false, any 3D shapes associated with the handle will no longer be hoverable even if 'Is3DHoverable' returns true.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoHandle.#ctor(RTG.Gizmo,System.Int32)
member.summary: Constructor.
member.param: The gizmo which owns the handle.
The handle id. Must be unique at gizmo scope.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoHandle.GetZoomFactor(UnityEngine.Camera)
member.summary: Returns the handle's zoom factor for the specified camera. This value can be used to scale handle shape properties such as sizes for example in order to allow the shapes to maintain a constant size regardless of the distance from the camera. This function uses the world position of the specified zoom factor transform to calculate the distance from the camera position. See also 'SetZoomFactorTransform'.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoHandle.SetZoomFactorTransform(RTG.GizmoTransform)
member.summary: Allows you to set the zoom factor transform. The world position of this transform is used by the 'GetZoomFactor' function to calculate the handle zoom factor. If null is specified, the function will set the zoom factor transform to the transform of the owner gizmo.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoHandle.SetHoverable(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Sets the hoverable state of the handle. This is equivalent to setting the 'Is2DHoverable' and 'Is3DHoverable' to the same value so it can be considered a shorthand function.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoHandle.SetVisible(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Sets the visibility state of the handle. This is equivalent to setting the 'Is2DVisible' and 'Is3DVisible' to the same value so it can be considered a shorthand function. Note: If this is set to false, the handle shapes will no longer be hoverable regardless of the values returned from 'Is2DHoverable' and 'Is3DHoverable'.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoHandle.Get3DShape(System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the 3D shape with the specified index.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoHandle.Get2DShape(System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the 2D shape with the specified index.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoHandle.SetAll3DShapesVisible(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Sets the visibility state of all the 3D shapes associated with the handle. This is equivalent to calling the 'Is3DVisible', but the 2 should not be confused. The reason why this function exists is because you can leave the 'Is3DVisible' property to true and hide shapes selectively. Later, you can call this function to restore the visibility state of all shapes as needed. Invisible shapes are also not hoverable.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoHandle.Set3DShapeVisible(System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Allows you to set the visibility state of the 3D shape with the specified index. Not to be confused with 'Is3DVisible'. 'Is3DVisible' can be used to toggle the visibility state of the handle whereas this function can be used to toggle the visibility state of a certain shape that belongs to the handle. Note: If 'Is3DVisible' is false, the visibility state of the 3D shapes is ignored and all 3D shapes will be considered invisible. Invisible shapes are also not hoverable.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoHandle.Is3DShapeVisible(System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the visibility state of the 3D shape with the specified index. Not to be confused with 'Is3DVisible'. For example, 'Is3DVisible' can return true, but this function will return false if a previous call to 'Set3DShapeVisible' was made to hide the shape.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoHandle.Set3DShapeHoverable(System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Allows you to set the hoverable state of the 3D shape with the specified index. Not to be confused with 'Is3DHoverable'. 'Is3DHoverable' can be used to toggle the hoverable state of the handle whereas this function can be used to toggle the hoverable state of a certain shape that belongs to the handle. Note: If 'Is3DHoverable' is false, the hoverable state of the 3D shapes is ignored and all 3D shapes will be considered NOT hoverable. Invisible shapes are not hoverable. So if the same shape was marked as invisible or if 'Is3DVisible' returns false, the shape is not hoverable.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoHandle.SetAll2DShapesVisible(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Sets the visibility state of all the 2D shapes associated with the handle. This is equivalent to calling the 'Is2DVisible', but the 2 should not be confused. The reason why this function exists is because you can leave the 'Is2DVisible' property to true and hide shapes selectively. Later, you can call this function to restore the visibility state of all shapes as needed. Invisible shapes are also not hoverable.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoHandle.Set2DShapeVisible(System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Allows you to set the visibility state of the 2D shape with the specified index. Not to be confused with 'Is2DVisible'. 'Is2DVisible' can be used to toggle the visibility state of the handle whereas this function can be used to toggle the visibility state of a certain shape that belongs to the handle. Note: If 'Is2DVisible' is false, the visibility state of the 2D shapes is ignored and all 2D shapes will be considered invisible. Invisible shapes are also not hoverable.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoHandle.Is2DShapeVisible(System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the visibility state of the 2D shape with the specified index. Not to be confused with 'Is2DVisible'. For example, 'Is2DVisible' can return true, but this function will return false if a previous call to 'Set2DShapeVisible' was made to hide the shape.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoHandle.Set2DShapeHoverable(System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Allows you to set the hoverable state of the 2D shape with the specified index. Not to be confused with 'Is2DHoverable'. 'Is2DHoverable' can be used to toggle the hoverable state of the handle whereas this function can be used to toggle the hoverable state of a certain shape that belongs to the handle. Note: If 'Is2DHoverable' is false, the hoverable state of the 2D shapes is ignored and all 2D shapes will be considered NOT hoverable. Invisible shapes are not hoverable. So if the same shape was marked as invisible or if 'Is2DVisible' returns false, the shape is not hoverable.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoHandle.Contains3DShape(RTG.Shape3D)
member.summary: Checks if the handle contains the specified 3D shape.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoHandle.Contains2DShape(RTG.Shape2D)
member.summary: Checks if the handle contains the specified 2D shape.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoHandle.Add3DShape(RTG.Shape3D)
member.summary: Adds the specified 3D shape to the handle.
member.returns: The index of the 3D shape inside the handle's 3D shape collection or -1 if the shape already exists.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoHandle.Add2DShape(RTG.Shape2D)
member.summary: Adds the specified 2D shape to the handle.
member.returns: The index of the 2D shape inside the handle's 2D shape collection or -1 if the shape already exists.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoHandle.Remove3DShape(RTG.Shape3D)
member.summary: Removes the specifed 3D shape. The function has no effect if the handle doesn't contain the specified shape.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoHandle.Remove2DShape(RTG.Shape2D)
member.summary: Removes the specified 2D shape. The function has no effect if the handle doesn't contain the specified shape.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoHandle.Render3DSolid
member.summary: Renders all 3D shapes as solids. This function will have no effect if 'Is3DVisible' returns false. Also, the function will ignore shapes which have been marked as invisible.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoHandle.Render3DWire
member.summary: Renders all 3D shapes using a wire representation. This function will have no effect if 'Is3DVisible' returns false. Also, the function will ignore shapes which have been marked as invisible.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoHandle.Render3DSolid(System.Int32)
member.summary: Renders the 3D shape with the specified index as a solid. The function has no effect if 'Is3DVisible' returns false or if the shape is invisible.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoHandle.Render3DWire(System.Int32)
member.summary: Renders the 3D shape with the specified index using a wire representation. The function has no effect if 'Is3DVisible' returns false or if the shape is invisible.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoHandle.Render2DSolid(UnityEngine.Camera)
member.summary: Renders all 2D shapes as solids. This function will have no effect if 'Is2DVisible' returns false. Also, the function will ignore shapes which have been marked as invisible.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoHandle.Render2DWire(UnityEngine.Camera)
member.summary: Renders all 2D shapes using a wire representation. This function will have no effect if 'Is2DVisible' returns false. Also, the function will ignore shapes which have been marked as invisible.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoHandle.Render2DSolid(UnityEngine.Camera,System.Int32)
member.summary: Renders the 2D shape with the specified index as a solid. The function has no effect if 'Is2DVisible' returns false or if the shape is invisible.
member.param: The camera which renders the shape.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoHandle.Render2DWire(UnityEngine.Camera,System.Int32)
member.summary: Renders the 2D shape with the specified index using a wire representation. The function has no effect if 'Is2DVisible' returns false or if the shape is invisible.
member.param: The camera which renders the shape.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoHandle.GetHoverData(UnityEngine.Ray)
member.summary: This function traverses the handle's 2D and 3D shapes and decides which one is hovered by the specified ray. It then returns the hover information inside an instance of the 'GizmoHandleHoverData' class. The ray should be created using the 'Camera.ScreenPointToRay' function and it represents the ray which is cast out from the screen into the 3D scene. The function will always give priority to 2D shapes. So for example, if the handle has a 2D and a 3D shape, and the ray hovers both of them, only the 2D shape will be taken into account.
member.param: The hover ray. This should be created using the 'Camera.ScreenPointToRay' function. The function will convert the origin of the ray in screen space to detect the 2D shapes which are hovered by the ray.
member.returns: If a shape is hovered by the input ray, the function returns an instance of the 'GizmoHandleHoverData' class. Otherwise, it returns null. The function also returns null if there are any subscribers to the 'CanHover' event that don't allow the handle to be hovered.

member.name: T:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D
member.summary: This class represents a 3D line slider. This type of slider is rendered as a straight line segment (thin or thick) and it allows for moving, rotating and scaling entities.

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.Direction
member.summary: Returns the slider's direction. This is a normalized axis which points from the slider's start point to its end point. When the slider is used for scaling, it is recommended to use 'GetRealDirection' instead because the scale session can cause the slider to point in the opposite direction. This is something that is ignored by this property.

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.DragRotationAxis
member.summary: Returns the rotation axis which is used during rotation sessions.

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.ScaleDragAxisIndex
P:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.StartPosition
member.summary: Returns the slider's starting position.

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.DragChannel
member.summary: Returns the slider's drag channel. This can be used to inform the client code about the type of drag that can be performed with the slider (move, rotate or scale).

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.IsDragged
member.summary: Checks if the slideer is currently being dragged. In order to get more specific info about how the slider is being dragged, use the 'IsMoving', 'IsRotating' and 'IsScaling' properties. The 'DragChannel' property can also be used to identify the type of drag that is being applied.

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.IsMoving
member.summary: Checks if the slider is being moved.

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.IsRotating
member.summary: Checks if the slider is being rotated.

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.IsScaling
member.summary: Checks if the slider is being scaled.

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.Is3DCapVisible
member.summary: Checks if the slider's 3D cap is visible.

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.Is3DCapHoverable
member.summary: Checks if the slider's 3D cap is hoverable.

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.Cap3DHandleId
member.summary: Returns the id of the cap handle.

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.TotalDragOffset
member.summary: Returns the total drag offset since the drag session started.

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.RelativeDragOffset
member.summary: Returns the relative drag offset since the last drag session update.

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.TotalDragRotation
member.summary: Returns the total drag rotation since the drag session started.

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.RelativeDragRotation
member.summary: Returns the relative drag rotation since the last drag session update.

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.TotalDragScale
member.summary: Returns the total drag scale since the drag session started.

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.RelativeDragScale
member.summary: Returns the relative drag scale since the last drag session update.

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.OverrideColor
member.summary: Returns the slider's override color. This can be used in situations where more direct control is required over the slider's color. This property only affects the slider's color. The cap color can not be affected by this property. In order to specify an override color for the slider's cap, you can use 'Cap3DOverrideColor'.

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.Cap3DOverrideColor
member.summary: Returns the override color for the slider's 3D cap. This can be used in situations where more direct control is required over the cap's color.

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.Settings
member.summary: Returns the slider's functional settings. If the slider uses shared settings, the shared settings will be returned. Otherwise, the property will return the settings associated with the slider instance.

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.SharedSettings
member.summary: Allows the client code to retrieve or set the slider's shared functional settings.

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.LookAndFeel
member.summary: Returns the slider's look and feel settings. If the slider uses shared settings, the shared settings will be returned. Otherwise, the property will return the settings associated with the slider instance.

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.SharedLookAndFeel
member.summary: Allows the client code to retrieve or set the slider's shared look and feel settings.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.#ctor(RTG.Gizmo,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Constructor.
member.param: The gizmo which owns the slider.
The id of the slider handle.
The id of the slider's cap handle.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.IsScalerHandleRegistered(System.Int32)
member.summary: Checks if the slider contains a record of a scaler handle with the specified id.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.IsScalerHandleRegistered(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Checks if the slider contains a record of a scaler handle with the specified id which also affects the specified scale drag axis.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.RegisterScalerHandle(System.Int32,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.Int32})
member.summary: This function is useful when the slider needs to have its size affected by scale sessions which originate from other handles. For example, the mid cap of a scale gizmo affects the length of the axes sliders. The function receives the id of the scaler handle (mid cap in this example) and a list of scale drag axis indices which represent the indices of the scale drag vector which are affected by the scaler handle. The function has no effect if a scaler handle with the same id already exists.
member.param: The scaler handle id.

A collection of scale drag axis indices which represent the indices of the scale drag vector components which are affected by the scaler handle. The important thing to remember is that the slider is only affected by the scale drag vector component with index = ScaleDragAxisIndex.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.UnregisterScalerHandle(System.Int32)
member.summary: Unregisters the scaler handle with the specified handle id. The function has no effect if a scaler handle with the specified id doesn't exist. After this function is called, any scale sessions which originate from the handle with the specified id will no longer affect the slider length.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.SetSnapEnabled(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Can be used to enable/disable snapping for all drag channels.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.Set3DCapVisible(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Sets the visibility state of the 3D cap. A visible cap will be rendered, and it can also be hovered if it is set to be hoverable (see 'Set3DCapHoverable').

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.Set3DCapHoverable(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Sets the hoverable state of the 3D cap. A hoverable cap can be hovered ONLY if it is visible (see 'Set3DCapVisible'). So passing true to this function will only allow the cap to be hovered if it is also visible.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.SetZoomFactorTransform(RTG.GizmoTransform)
member.summary: Sets the zoom factor transform. The world position of the zoom factor transform is used to calculate a slider zoom factor. The zoom factor is used in order to allow the slider to maintain a constant size regardless of its distance from the camera.
member.param: The zoom factor transform. If null, the function will default to using the transform of the owner gizmo instead.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.GetZoomFactor(UnityEngine.Camera)
member.summary: Calculates and returns the slider zoom factor for the specified camera. The zoom factor is calculated using the current zoom factor transform. See 'SetZoomFactorTransform'. This value can be useful for scaling different values such as sizes for example in order to allow them to have a constant magnitude in relation to the distance between the camera and the position of the zoom factor transform. The function returns a value of 1.0f if the look and feel settings have been set up to ignore the zoom factor.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.GetRealDirection
member.summary: Returns the real direction axis of the slider. This function has the same purpose as the 'Direction' property, but it also takes into account the scaling which is applied during a scale session which can cause the slider to point in the opposite direction. If you know that a slider will never be used for scaling, you can use the 'Direction' property instead as it is faster.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.GetRealSizeAlongDirection(UnityEngine.Camera,UnityEngine.Vector3)
member.summary: Returns the size (length and thickness) of the slider along the specified direction axis. The function will take into accoun the look and feel settings such as the slider type (line, box, cylinder), scale and whether or not a zoom factor should be applied.
member.param: This is the camera needed to calculate the slider zoom factor if necessary.
The direction along which the slider size will be calculated.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.GetRealLength(System.Single)
member.summary: Returns the real length of the slider. The real length takes into account all the look and feel settings such as length, scale and zoom factor and combines them to return a value that represents the real length of the slider. If the slider is participating in a scale drag session, the total scale applied since session begin will also be used to calculate the length.
member.param: The slider's zoom factor. Can be calculated using 'GetZoomFactor'.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.GetRealLengthWith3DCap(System.Single)
member.summary: Same as 'GetRealLength', but it also takes the length of the 3D cap into account.
member.param: The slider's zoom factor. Can be calculated using 'GetZoomFactor'.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.GetRealEndPosition(System.Single)
member.summary: Returns the slider's real end point. Works in pretty much the same way as 'GetRealLength' but it can be used to calculate the end point instead. In fact this function uses 'GetRealLength' under the hood to travel from the start poition to the end.
member.param: The slider's zoom factor. Can be calculated using 'GetZoomFactor'.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.GetRealEndPositionWith3DCap(System.Single)
member.summary: Same as 'GetRealEndPosition' but it also takes into account the slider's 3D cap.
member.param: The slider's zoom factor. Can be calculated using 'GetZoomFactor'.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.GetRealBoxHeight(System.Single)
member.summary: Returns the real height of the slider's box. This is useful when the slider type is set to box and you need to know the real height of the box. The real height takes into account scale and zoom factor if necessary.
member.param: The slider's zoom factor. Can be calculated using 'GetZoomFactor'.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.GetRealBoxDepth(System.Single)
member.summary: Returns the real depth of the slider's box. This is useful when the slider type is set to box and you need to know the real depth of the box. The real depth takes into account scale and zoom factor if necessary.
member.param: The slider's zoom factor. Can be calculated using 'GetZoomFactor'.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.GetRealCylinderRadius(System.Single)
member.summary: Returns the real radius of the slider's cylinder. This is useful when the slider type is set to cylinder and you need to know the real radius. The real radius takes into account scale and zoom factor if necessary.
member.param: The slider's zoom factor. Can be calculated using 'GetZoomFactor'.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.MapDirection(System.Int32,RTG.AxisSign)
member.summary: This function can be used to link the slider's direction axis to one its transform axes. For example, if you wish the direction axis to always point along the slider's transform up vector, you would call MapDirection(1, AxisSign.Positive). Linking the direction axis to the slider's transform like this has the advantage that no matter how the slider is rotated, the direction axis will follow. The function has no effect if the slider is currently involved in a drag operation.
member.param: The index of the slider transform axis which will act as the direction axis. The format is: 0->X, 1->Y, 2->Z.
The sign of the axis. Allows you to differentiate between positive and negative axes.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.MapDragRotationAxis(RTG.GizmoTransform,System.Int32,RTG.AxisSign)
member.summary: Similar to 'MapDirection', but this function applies to the slider's drag rotation axis. This is the axis around which the slider will rotate during a rotation session. The other difference is that this function requires you to pass a transform and the rest of the parameters identify the axis inside this transform. The function has no effect if the slider is currently involved in a drag operation.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.UnmapDragRotationAxis
member.summary: Removes the mapping for the drag rotation axis which was created using a previous call to 'MapDragRotationAxis'. The function has no effect if the slider is currently involved in a drag operation.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.SetDirection(UnityEngine.Vector3)
member.summary: Sets the direction of the slider. This is useful when you have access to a direction vector and you would like the slider to point along this vector. Calling this function will change the slider transform's rotation in order to align the direction axis with the desired vector. The function has no effect if the slider is currently involved in a drag operation.
member.param: The slider's direction axis. The function will automatically normalize this vector.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.SetDragRotationAxis(UnityEngine.Vector3)
member.summary: Sets the drag rotation axis. This is useful when you have access to a rotation axis vector and you would like to use that during rotation sessions. Calling this function will cancel the mapping created via a previous call to 'MapDragRotationAxis'. The function has no effect if the slider is currently involved in a drag operation.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.AddTargetTransform(RTG.GizmoTransform)
member.summary: Call this function when you have a transform that you would like to be affected by the slider's drag sessions. After calling this function, the world position and rotation of the specified transform will be affected by the slider's offset and rotation drag sessions. The function has no effect if the transform has already been registered via a previous call to 'AddTargetTransform'.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.AddTargetTransform(RTG.GizmoTransform,RTG.GizmoDragChannel)
member.summary: Call this function when you have a transform that you would like to be affected by one of the slider's drag sessions. The specified drag channel identifies the type of session that will affect the specified transform. The function has no effect if the transform has already been registered via a previous call to 'AddTargetTransform'.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.RemoveTargetTransform(RTG.GizmoTransform)
member.summary: Removes the specified target transform. After this function is called, the specified transform will no longer be affected by any of the slider's drag sessions.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.RemoveTargetTransform(RTG.GizmoTransform,RTG.GizmoDragChannel)
member.summary: Removes the specified target transform. After this function is called, the specified transform will no longer be affected by the drag session which corresponds to the specified drag channel.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.SetDragChannel(RTG.GizmoDragChannel)
member.summary: Sets the slider's drag channel. This is how the client code can switch between an offset slider, a rotation slider or a scale slider.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.ApplyZoomFactor(UnityEngine.Camera)
member.summary: This function calculates the slider's zoom factor for the specified camera and uses it to adjust the slider shape transforms such as size for example. The zoom factor is automatically applied during each gizmo 'UpdateBegin' step, so the ONLY time when this function should be called is when more than one camera can render the slider. In that case, before calling 'Render, the client code should call this function to ensure that the slider is rendered correctly. The function has no effect if the look and feel settings' 'UseZoomFactor' property returns false.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.Render(UnityEngine.Camera)
member.summary: Renders the slider with the specified camera. This function should be called directly or indirectly from a Monobehaviour's 'OnRenderObject' function.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoLineSlider3D.Refresh
member.summary: This function can be called after changing functional or look and feel settings to ensure that these settings take effect.

member.name: T:RTG.GizmoSlider
member.summary: Abstract slider base class which can be derived to define behaviours for different types of sliders. A slider is a gizmo entity that allows for moving, rotating and/or scaling entities.

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoSlider.Gizmo
member.summary: Returns the gizmo that owns the slider handle.

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoSlider.HandleId
member.summary: Returns the id of the slider handle.

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoSlider.IsVisible
member.summary: Checks if the slider is visible.

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoSlider.IsHoverable
member.summary: Checks if the slider is hoverable.

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoSlider.IsHovered
member.summary: Checks if the slider is hovered.

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoSlider.HoverPriority3D
member.summary: Returns the slider's 3D hover priority. This property is only useful when the slider uses 3D shapes.

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoSlider.HoverPriority2D
member.summary: Returns the slider's 2D hover priority. This property is only useful when the slider uses 2D shapes.

member.name: P:RTG.GizmoSlider.GenericHoverPriority
member.summary: Returns the slider's generic hover priority.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoSlider.#ctor(RTG.Gizmo,System.Int32)
member.summary: Constructor.
member.param: The gizmo which owns the slider.
The id of the slider handle.

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoSlider.SetVisible(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Sets the visibility state of the slider. A visible slider will be rendered, and it can also be hovered if it is set to be hoverable (see 'SetHoverable').

member.name: M:RTG.GizmoSlider.SetHoverable(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Sets the hoverable state of the slider. A hoverable slider can be hovered ONLY if it is visible (see 'SetVisible'). So passing true to this function will only allow the slider to be hovered if it is also visible.

member.name: M:RTG.MoveGizmo.UpdateViewParallelVisibility(UnityEngine.Camera)
member.summary: Computes desired visibility for move handles (LookAndFeel baseline combined with view-parallel hiding) and applies only the changes. Cheap when nothing changes.

member.name: M:RTG.UniversalGizmo.UpdateMvViewParallelVisibility(UnityEngine.Camera)
member.summary: Computes desired visibility for move handles (LookAndFeel baseline combined with view-parallel hiding) and applies only the changes. Cheap when nothing changes.

member.name: M:RTG.SphereTreeNode`1.IsOutsideParent
member.summary: Checks if the node's sphere is outside of its parent's sphere. The method returns false if the node doesn't have a parent.

member.name: M:RTG.SphereTreeNode`1.ClosestChild(RTG.SphereTreeNode{`0})
member.summary: Finds the child which is closest to 'node'.
member.returns: The child closest to 'node' or null if the no children are available.

member.name: M:RTG.SphereTreeNode`1.SetParent(RTG.SphereTreeNode{`0})
member.summary: Sets the node's parent. This call is ignored if the specified parent is the node itself or if it's the same as the current parent.

member.name: M:RTG.SphereTreeNode`1.EncapsulateChildrenBottomUp
member.summary: This method will recalculate the node's center and radius so that it encapsulates all children. This is a recursive call which propagates up the hierarchy towards the root.

member.name: M:RTG.SphereTreeNode`1.DebugDraw
member.summary: Allows the node to render itself for debugging purposes. The client code is responsible for setting up the rendering material.
member.remarks: This method is recursive and will draw the node's children also. Thus, it is enough to call this method for the root of a sphere tree in order to draw the entire tree.

member.name: M:RTG.CameraViewVolume.GetNearPlanePoints
member.summary: Returns a list of all points which make up the near plane. The points are stored in the following order: top-left, top-right, bottom-right, bottom-left.

member.name: M:RTG.CameraViewVolume.CalculateWorldPoints(UnityEngine.Camera)
member.summary: Calculates the volume world space points for the specified camera. Must be called after the world planes have been calculated.

member.name: F:RTG.ColorTransition.State.CompleteFadeIn
member.summary: This is the state the transition exists in after it has completed a fade-in operation.

member.name: F:RTG.ColorTransition.State.CompleteFadeOut
member.summary: This is the state the transition exists in after it has completed a fade-out operation.

member.name: F:RTG.ColorTransition.State.FadingIn
member.summary: The transition is fading in.

member.name: F:RTG.ColorTransition.State.FadingOut
member.summary: The transition is fading out.

member.name: F:RTG.ColorTransition.State.Ready
member.summary: The transition is in a ready state. This is the state in which the transition exists when it is created. After a fade is performed, it can never go back to this state.

member.name: M:RTG.GameObjectEx.GetGameObjectType(UnityEngine.GameObject)
member.summary: Returns the game object's type.

member.name: M:RTG.GraphicsEx.DrawWireCornerBox(RTG.OBB,System.Single)
member.summary: Renders a wire box with lines meeting at corners.
member.param: Can have values in the [0, 1] interval and it controls the length of the corner lines. A value of 0 draws lines of length 0 and a value of 1 draws lines of length equal to half the box length along a certain axis.

member.name: M:RTG.RectEx.GetCornerPoints(UnityEngine.Rect)
member.summary: Returns a list with the rectangle's corner points. The elements inside this list have a one-to-one maping with members of the 'RectCornerPoint' enum.

member.name: M:RTG.RectEx.PlaceBelowCenterHrz(UnityEngine.Rect,UnityEngine.Rect)
member.summary: Places 'rect' below 'other' and sets its center to the same center as 'other' horizontally.

member.name: M:RTG.RectEx.InvertScreenY(UnityEngine.Rect)
member.summary: Inverts the rectangle's Y coordinates in screen space. Useful when the rectangle needs to be expressed in coordinate systems with the Y axis going either up or down.

member.name: M:RTG.SpriteRendererEx.GetWorldCenterPoint(UnityEngine.SpriteRenderer)
member.summary: Returns the sprite's world center point. This function assumes that the sprite renderer has a valid sprite associated with it.

member.name: M:RTG.SpriteRendererEx.GetModelSpaceSize(UnityEngine.SpriteRenderer)
member.summary: Returns the sprite's model space size. This function assumes that the sprite renderer has a valid sprite associated with it.

member.name: M:RTG.SpriteRendererEx.GetModelSpaceAABB(UnityEngine.SpriteRenderer)
member.summary: Returns the sprite's model space AABB. The function will return an invalid AABB if the sprite renderer doesn't have a sprite associated with it.

member.name: M:RTG.SpriteRendererEx.IsPixelFullyTransparent(UnityEngine.SpriteRenderer,UnityEngine.Vector3)
member.summary: Checks if the sprite pixel which resides at 'worldPos' is fully transparent.
member.remarks: Works only when the Read/Write enabled flag is set inside the sprite texture properties. Otherwise, it will always return false.

member.name: M:RTG.Hotkeys.HasNoModifiers
member.summary: Checks if the shortcut has any modifiers assigned to it.

member.name: M:RTG.Hotkeys.HasNoMouseButtons
member.summary: Checks if the shortcut has any mouse buttons assigned to it.

member.name: M:RTG.Hotkeys.IsEmpty
member.summary: Checks if no keys, mouse buttons or modifier keys are available for this shortcut.

member.name: M:RTG.Hotkeys.IsAnyModifierKeyPressed
member.summary: Checks if at least one modifier key is pressed.

member.name: M:RTG.Hotkeys.IsAnyMouseButtonPressed
member.summary: Checks if at least one mouse button is pressed.

member.name: M:RTG.OBB.GetPointFaceNormal(UnityEngine.Vector3)
member.summary: Assuming 'pointOnFace' is a point which resides on one of the OBB's faces, the method will return the normal of this face.

member.name: T:RTG.PlaneQuadrantId
member.summary: Identifies different quadrants in which a plane can exist. The quadrants go from 'First' to 'Fourth' in counter clockwise order when looking down the plane normal.

member.name: M:RTG.Sphere.Encapsulate(RTG.Sphere)
member.summary: Encapsulates the specified sphere. This method will adjust the sphere's center and radius to include 'sphere'. The method has no effect if the passed sphere is aready encapsulated by 'this' sphere.

member.name: M:RTG.MeshTree.RaycastClosest(UnityEngine.Ray,UnityEngine.Matrix4x4)
member.summary: Performs a raycast against the mesh triangles and returns info about the closest hit or null if no triangle was hit by the ray.
member.param: The mesh transform which brings the mesh in the same space as the ray.
member.remarks: This method will build the tree if it hasn't already been built.

member.name: M:RTG.RTMesh.Create(UnityEngine.Mesh)
member.summary: Factory function for creating an RTMesh instance from the specified Unity mesh. The function will return null if the RTMesh can not be created. Such a scenario occurs when the Unity mesh is not readable.
member.remarks: The client code is responsible for calling 'BuildTree' for the returned mesh.

member.name: M:RTG.RTMeshDb.GetRTMesh(UnityEngine.Mesh)
member.summary: Given a Unity mesh, the method will return the associated RTMesh. The method will attempt to create an RTMesh instance if one doesn't exist for the passed Unity mesh. If no RTMesh is associated with the Unity mesh and if one can not be created, null will be returned.
member.remarks: The client code is responsible for calling 'BuildTree' for the returned mesh. This would only be necessary if the method had to create a new RTMesh instance in case one didn't already exist.

member.name: M:RTG.RTMeshDb.CreateRTMesh(UnityEngine.Mesh)
member.summary: Creates an RTMesh from the passed Unity mesh. The method returns the RTMesh instance or null if the mesh can not be created.
member.remarks: The client code is responsible for calling 'BuildTree' for the returned mesh.

member.name: F:RTG.GameObjectType.Empty
member.summary: Identifies an empty object. An empty object is an object which doesn't belong to any of the types above.

member.name: F:RTG.XZGridLookAndFeel._useCellFading
member.summary: If this is true, the grid cells will fade in/out based on the distance between the grid and the camera. This is consistent with how Unity renders the scene grid inside the Editor.

member.name: F:RTG.RTCustomObjectInteractionSettings._noVolumeObjectSize
member.summary: The custom interaction system needs to know the size of objects that have no volume. This defines a volume for these objects in the 3D world so that they can still be involved in raycasts, overlap tests etc.

member.name: F:RTG.SceneRaycastPrecision.BestFit
member.summary: If the object has a mesh, the raycast will be performed against the object mesh surface. If the object doesn't have a mesh, but it has a terrain with a terrain collider, it will be performed against the terrain collider. If none of these are available, the raycast will be performed against the object's volume/box.

member.name: F:RTG.SceneRaycastPrecision.Box
member.summary: The raycast will always be performed against the object's volume/box.

member.name: P:RTG.MonoSingleton`1.Get
member.summary: Returns the singleton instance.
member.remarks: If no instance of the required MonoBehaviour type exists in the scene, the property will return null. Null is also returned if more than one instance is found.

member.name: M:HACK_TextLayoutFix.Apply(UnityEngine.UIElements.VisualElement)
member.summary: Works around a Unity bug with inconsistent text wrapping, especially prevalent in Chinese. Source: https://forum.unity.com/threads/text-wrap-bug.913379/ Bug reported: https://unity3d.atlassian.net/servicedesk/customer/portal/2/IN-73456

member.name: T:CoroutineHost
member.summary: Allows running co-routines from any part of the program. Must be invoked from the main thread.
